item.title: Best AMD Ryzen laptops in 2023
item.itunes:title: Best AMD Ryzen laptops in 2023
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Typically, Intel processors have been much more prevalent than AMD ones, especially in laptops. Even today, many of the best laptops you can buy are powered by Intel processors. However, since introducing the Ryzen family, AMD has made great strides in becoming more popular, and more and more fantastic laptops have shown up with AMD Ryzen processors inside.

These days, you can find really great laptops powered by AMD Ryzen in all different segments of the market. Whether you want a new gaming rig, a premium laptop, or something to get work done, AMD processors are now at the heart of many devices, and they're some of the best you can find, too. If you're looking to upgrade to a new AMD laptop, we've rounded up the best options you can find today. As we've mentioned, these laptops cover a lot of ground, so no matter what you're looking for, you're bound to find something you like here.

  • Acer Swift Edge 16 (2023)
    Best overall
    $1300 at Best Buy
  • Alienware m16 (AMD)
    Best gaming laptop
    $1700 at Dell
  • MSI Bravo 15
    Best budget gaming laptop
    $923 at Walmart
  • Dell Inspiron 14 2-in-1
    Best budget convertible
    $550 at Dell (AMD)
  • Lenovo Yoga 7 (16-inch, 2023)
    Best convertible
    $640 at Lenovo
  • Source: Amazon
    ASUS Vivobook Pro 15
    Best laptops for creators
    $1400 at Amazon
  • HP Envy x360 15.6 (2023)
    Best business laptop
    $800 at Best Buy
  • MSI Modern 15
    Best budget
    $700 at Amazon

The top AMD Ryzen laptops in 2023

Acer Swift Edge 16 (2023)
Best overall

Lightweight and fast with a stunning OLED display

The 2023 Acer Swift Edge comes with AMD Ryzen 7000 series processors and powerful Nvidia RTX 4000-series graphics cards. Additionally, it packs a beautiful OLED display with a 3.2K resolution and a 120Hz refresh rate.

Pros
  • Fast AMD Ryzen 7 7040 processors
  • Stunning 120Hz OLED display
  • Extremely thin and light for its size
Cons
  • Lack of true 4K resolution
$1300 at Best Buy $1600 at B&H

The Acer Swift Edge was already one of our favorite thin and lightweight laptops, and the 2023 model improves upon its predecessor's design in almost every way except the display resolution.

Performance-wise, it's packing an AMD Ryzen 7 7840U processor with 8 cores and 16 threads for excellent performance. This processor also includes Radeon 780M integrated graphics for solid performance in 3D workloads. Plus, 16GB of LPDDR5 RAM and up to a 2TB SSD round out a top-tier set of specs.

The laptop has a 16-inch OLED panel with a refresh rate of 120Hz, making it incredibly smooth and vibrant. Above that display is a 1440p webcam, which is always great to see. However, the laptop has a 3200x2000 display resolution, which is lower than the 4K resolution offered by its predecessor, though still more than sharp enough for this size.

Despite the powerful processor and fantastic display, the Acer Swift Edge 16 is extremely light for a 16-inch laptop, coming in at a mere 2.7 pounds thanks to the magnesium alloy chassis. Even with this thin and lightweight design, you get a lot of ports, including two USB Type-C, two USB Type-A, an HDMI, and a headphone jack. This makes for a well-rounded setup, especially considering the device's thinness. You should be able to use most of your peripherals with it without needing adapters, which is excellent. Acer really knocked it out of the park with the Swift Edge 16, and it's one of the best laptops you can buy in general, but especially if you want an AMD Ryzen processor.

Alienware m16 (AMD)
Best gaming laptop

Extreme performance for demanding games

The Alienware m16 is a super-powerful gaming laptop packing AMD Ryzen 7045HX series processors and Nvidia GeForce RTX 40 series GPUs. It also has a large 16-inch screen with a 16:10 aspect ratio, great for gaming and content creation.

Pros
  • Top-tier AMD Ryzen CPUs and Nvidia GeForce GPUs
  • Tall 16:10 display is great for gaming and content creation
  • Sleek Alienware design language
Cons
  • Very thick and heavy
  • Expensive
$1700 at Dell $2800 at Best Buy

AMD processors have always been more popular in gaming laptops, and if that's what you're looking for, the Alienware m16 is the laptop for you. This is one of the fastest laptops on the market, starting with powerful AMD Ryzen 7045HX processors, up to a Ryzen 9 7945HX with 12 cores and 24 threads. If that's not wild enough, the laptop also comes with up to an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4080 (a 4090 model will come later) GPU, and these two components can use up to 240W of power combined. Rounding things out, you can go up to 64GB of RAM and a massive 8.5TB of storage.

The 16-inch display comes in a 16:10 aspect ratio, making this a super laptop for gaming and productivity. The base model has Quad HD+ resolution and a 165Hz refresh rate, but you can upgrade to a 240Hz panel that also covers 100% of DCI-P3 for nicer colors overall. If you value frame rates more, there's also a Full HD+ 480Hz panel for maximum smoothness in esports titles. Above the display is a 1080p webcam with optional Windows Hello support, depending on your chosen display.

The Alienware m16 features the company's signature design language, with an all-black chassis and RGB lighting on the keyboard and around the exhaust vents on the back. Of course, given the powerful specs, it's fairly thick and heavy, going up to 7.28 pounds depending on the specs you choose. As usual with gaming laptops, ports are plentiful, with two USB Type-C and two Type-A ports, HDMI, Mini DisplayPort, RJ45 Ethernet (2.5Gbps), a headphone jack, and a full-size SD card reader.

You'll have to pay a pretty penny for the privilege of owning the Alienware m16, but it's a powerful laptop that won't let you down if you want to run the latest games.

MSI Bravo 15
Best budget gaming laptop

Solid gaming performance at a lower price

Armed with Nvidia's 40-series GPUs and AMD's Ryzen 7035 processors, the MSI Bravo 15 is a fantastic lineup of gaming laptops that's also light on your wallet. What's more, this budget gaming laptop packs an FHD display with a smooth 144Hz refresh rate.

Pros
  • Fast AMD 7035 processors and Nvidia 40 series GPUs
  • Smooth 144Hz refresh rate
  • Inexpensive for a gaming laptop
Cons
  • 720p webcam
  • Bulky
$923 at Walmart $1000 at Best Buy

AMD CPUs are known to deliver powerful performance at a low price, and AMD's Ryzen 7000 laptop processors are no exception. So, if you're looking for a cheap laptop that can play the latest games at high frame rates, look no further than the MSI Bravo 15, powered by these heavy-hitting CPUs.

From its specifications alone, it's clear that the MSI Bravo 15 is no slouch. The base model comes with a Ryzen 5 7535HS processor and is powered by Nvidia's RTX 4050 GPU, all while costing $800. If you want more performance, you can go for the pricier variant with a Ryzen 7 7735HS CPU and an RTX 4060. Both MSI Bravo 15 models have a 512GB PCIe NVMe SSD and offer a buttery smooth FHD 144Hz display for all your high-speed gaming needs. Factor in the blazing-fast 8GB DDR5 4800MHz memory, and these laptops can handle most modern games with ease.

One downside of this laptop is its bulky design. It weighs almost 5 pounds and has a 24.8-millimeter thickness, meaning it's not the most portable gaming laptop on the market. As for the ports, the MSI Bravo 15 houses all essential connections, including a USB Type-C connector, three USB Type-A ports, and one HDMI connector. It even comes with a headphone/microphone combo jack.

Unlike most gaming laptops, the MSI Bravo doesn't feature flashy aesthetics. Instead, the laptop sports a more professional look, making it great for users who want a sleek and powerful laptop for everyday work. While the MSI Bravo 15 may not be the very best laptop if you're a heavy gamer, it's a solid choice for a budget gaming device.

Dell Inspiron 14 2-in-1
Best budget convertible

Great performance for a lower price

A great convertible for those on a budget, the Dell Inspiron 14 is a mainstream 2-in-1 laptop that offers solid performance thanks to its AMD Ryzen 7030 processors. It also has a sleek build, a 1080p webcam, and a tall 16:10 display.

Pros
  • Fast and energy-efficient AMD Ryzen 7030 series processors
  • Solid display with a 1080p webcam
  • Decent port selection
Cons
  • No support for DDR5 memory
  • Low peak brightness of 250 nits
$550 at Dell (AMD) $700 at Best Buy (AMD)

If you want the versatility of a convertible without breaking the bank, Dell's Inspiron 14 is the one for you. We've reviewed multiple iterations of this budget 2-in-1 laptop in the past and loved every single one. Fortunately, the 2023 model is even better thanks to some performance improvements in the CPU department.

The latest Inspiron 14 laptop ships with AMD's Ryzen 7030 processors, and you can get up to a Ryzen 7 7730U with 8 cores and 16 threads. It seems like a minor upgrade at first, but these processors are more efficient and faster than the previous 2022 and 2021 models. While the lack of support for DDR5 memory can be a downer, the 16GB of LPDDR4X will suffice for most users. You also won't have to worry about running out of space as you can configure it up to 1TB of SSD space.

As for the display, you're looking at a 14-inch WVA panel with a 1920 x 1200 screen resolution and a 60Hz refresh rate. The touchscreen display has a tall 16:10 aspect ratio, making the Inspiron 14 a fantastic budget option for productivity-related tasks. At a peak brightness of 250 nits, it's not good enough to be used outdoors. Fortunately, you do get a 1080p front camera, which isn't something you'll observe in a laptop in this price range.

The Inspiron 14 rocks a sleek silver body and weighs 3.48 pounds, so it's not the lightest convertible on the market. With two USB Type-C ports, one USB Type-A connection, an SD card slot, an audio jack, and an HDMI port, the convertible provides decent connectivity for the average user.

All in all, the Dell Inspiron 14 is certainly one of the best laptops you can find with an AMD Ryzen processor. It's not aimed at the high-end of the market, but it's fantastic for just about everyone else.

Lenovo Yoga 7 (16-inch, 2023)
Best convertible

A versatile form factor with high-performance specs

The 2023 Lenovo Yoga 7 is a powerful convertible laptop with AMD Ryzen 7035 series processors, delivering strong performance in a versatile form factor. The 16-inch display comes in Full HD+ resolution, perfect for getting work done.

Pros
  • AMD Ryzen 7035 processors deliver strong performance
  • Large 16-inch display is great for multi-tasking
  • Plenty of ports
Cons
  • The display isn't very vibrant
  • Somewhat heavy
$640 at Lenovo $750 at Best Buy

If you want a laptop that's just a bit more versatile, the Lenovo Yoga 7 is a fantastic convertible laptop with high-end AMD Ryzen processors and a big canvas to work on.

Starting with performance, the Lenovo Yoga 7 (2023) is powered by AMD Ryzen 7035U series processors, delivering strong performance with up to 8 cores and 16 threads, plus Radeon 680M integrated graphics. With this GPU, it's actually possible to play a lot of games as long as you can tweak some settings. It can also be configured with up to 16GB of RAM and a 1TB SSD for a great experience all around.

The display isn't as impressive as the Acer Swift Edge, but it's still a large 16-inch panel with a 16:10 aspect ratio, so it's great for productivity. It comes in Full HD+ resolution, though you may find it disappointing that it only covers 45% of NTSC, so colors won't be super vibrant. Being a convertible means the screen supports touch and pen input, too. Above the display is a 1080p webcam with Windows Hello support, which is always nice to see.

In terms of design, the Lenovo Yoga 7 looks nice with its curved edges, especially in the Tidal Teal colorway, but the Storm Grey model is more widely available and a bit more boring. At 4.49 pounds, it's a bit on the heavier side, though it makes sense for an aluminum laptop. You do get a ton of ports, though, including two USB Type-C and two Type-A ports, HDMI, a headphone jack, and a microSD card reader. Unfortunately, the USB Type-C ports don't support USB4.

It's not quite as premium as some Intel-powered convertibles, but the Lenovo Yoga 7 is a great machine at a very reasonable price for what it offers.

Source: Amazon
ASUS Vivobook Pro 15
Best laptops for creators

Blazing-fast performance for creative professionals

Designed for creators, the ASUS Vivobook Pro 15 is a powerful yet affordable 15.6-inch laptop that offers tons of features for those into content creation. With up to a Ryzen 9 7940HS CPU and an RTX 4060 graphics card, you won't have to worry about running into performance issues either.

Pros
  • Ryzen 7000-series CPUs can crush most creative workloads
  • RTX 4000 GPUs are great for video editing and graphics rendering
  • 1080p webcam with privacy shutter
  • Better security with a fingerprint sensor built into the power key
Cons
  • Lack of an OLED panel can be a deal-breaker
  • 16:9 aspect is a bit lacking for productivity tasks
$1400 at Amazon $1400 at Best Buy

Looking for a sleek and powerful laptop for your creativity needs? The ASUS Vivobook Pro 15 is definitely the one you should get.

From a performance standpoint, the latest Vivobook Pro 15 laptop supports up to a Ryzen 9 7940HS processor, which provides unmatched performance with its 8 cores, 16 threads, and 5.2GHz boost clock. On the GPU front, you're looking at a highly capable RTX 4060 that can handle most video editing and rendering tasks with ease. You also get up to 32GB of LPDDR5X memory alongside a 1TB SSD for an all-round solid experience.

While the 15.6-inch FHD+ display isn't bad per se, it can be a bit lacking considering most Intel-based laptops in the Vivobook series feature OLED displays. Similarly, the 16:9 aspect ratio isn't very optimized for creators. Nevertheless, ASUS somewhat compensates for these drawbacks with a 144Hz refresh rate and an 84% screen-to-body ratio. As for the webcam, you're looking at a 1080p front camera with a built-in privacy shutter.

The Vivobook Pro 15 bears a robust, professional-looking chassis with a 180° hinge. At 3.97 pounds, it is a bit on the bulkier side, though that is to be expected considering its powerful hardware. Its backlit chiclet keyboard also has antimicrobial properties, and you even get a fingerprint sensor integrated into the power button for enhanced security. Finally, the laptop includes three USB Type-A ports, one USB Type-C port, an HDMI 2.1 connection, a 3.5mm audio jack, and an SD card reader, so it does cover most needs you might have.

Leaving aside its display limitations, the Vivobook Pro 15 is a good laptop in almost all respects, and it's hard to fault it considering its price point.

HP Envy x360 15.6 (2023)
Best business laptop

Top-tier performance and a sleek professional design

Compact and budget-friendly, the HP Envy x360 comes with AMD Ryzen 7030 series processors. It has a touchscreen display that supports pen input, and you can outfit this high-end business laptop with an OLED on HP's website.

Pros
  • Solid performance thanks to AMD Ryzen 7030 series processors
  • Sleek and modern design
  • Touchscreen support
Cons
  • Last-gen LPDDR4X memory
  • Rather bulky
$950 at HP (AMD) $800 at Best Buy

The HP Envy lineup has served the needs of professionals for more than a decade, and the 2023 is a worthy addition to the series.

Powering this high-end business laptop are AMD Ryzen 7030 series processors, up to a Ryzen 7 7730U processor with 8 cores and 16 threads. These processors deliver fantastic performance, and they feature integrated Radeon RX Vega 8 graphics, which deliver strong GPU performance for basic tasks. Since the LPDR4X RAM modules are soldered onto the motherboard, you can't upgrade them, though 16GB of memory should be enough for most business-oriented tasks. You also get up to 1TB worth of SSD storage.

The HP Envy x360 has a 15.6-inch display with a 16:9 aspect ratio, but it gives you a couple of configuration options. The base model comes in Full HD+ resolution (1920 x 1080) with touchscreen support, but you can upgrade to an OLED panel for a rich viewing experience. This panel is sharper and has much more vibrant colors thanks to OLED technology. HP has also equipped the laptop's 5MP HD webcam with IR sensors, so it's compatible with Windows Hello facial recognition.

Design-wise, the 2023 model of the Envy x360 follows the same formula of a compact and sleek-looking chassis as the last-gen models. At 4.03 pounds, it's on the bulkier side for a business laptop. It also has a decent collection of ports as you get two USB Type-C connections, one USB Type-A port, an HDMI connection, an SD card reader, and a headphone/microphone combo jack.

With its affordable price, the HP Envy x360 is easily the best business laptop with Team Red processors.

MSI Modern 15
Best budget

Solid specs at an affordable price

The MSI Modern 15 is a great budget laptop that's powered by AMD's Ryzen 7030 U-series processors and feature a large 15.6-inch Full HD display. Measuring 19.8 millimeters in thickness, it's also relatively compact, though it does weigh 5.6 pounds.

Pros
  • AMD's Ryzen 7030U processors offer solid performance
  • Plenty of ports
  • Affordable
Cons
  • 720p webcam
  • Doesn't support DDR5 memory
$700 at Amazon $700 at Newegg $700 at Walmart

If the rest of the laptops on this list seem a bit pricey, then you should consider checking out the MSI Modern 15. Its base model comes with the 6-core AMD Ryzen 7530U processor that has base and turbo clock speeds of 2.00GHz and 4.50GHz, respectively. If that sounds underwhelming, you can go for the slightly pricier variant, which offers the better-performing Ryzen 7730U CPU. While the lack of support for DDR5 memory can be seen as a drawback, the laptop's 16GB DDR4 RAM works well for day-to-day tasks, and its 1920x1080 display resolution and 16:9 aspect ratio are good enough for the price. You're also covered on the storage front as the laptop includes a fast 1TB NVMe SSD that you won't need to upgrade for a long time.

Weighing around 3.85 pounds, the MSI Modern 15 certainly isn't as light as the HP Pavilion Aero 13 (2023), but it's not as heavy as a typical budget laptop. As for the ports, the laptop has one HDMI slot, a headphone jack, one USB Type-C connection, and three USB Type-A ports.

Its webcam, however, isn't particularly great. The 720p sensor on the webcam can cause poor video quality, so you're better off spending some cash on a dedicated webcam instead of using the laptop's built-in camera. But considering that the MSI Modern 15 starts at $700, it's hard not to recommend this budget-friendly laptop.

Best AMD Ryzen laptops in 2023: The bottom line

That's it for this list! You really can't go wrong with any of these laptops, but if we had to pick the best one from the bunch, it'd certainly be the Acer Swift Edge 16. It is extremely light, yet it has a large and sharp 4K display with stunning OLED technology. The high-performance processor on the Acer Swift Edge 16 is just the icing on the cake. You can't ask for much more here, even if the processor inside isn't the newest around anymore.

Acer Swift Edge 16 (2023)
Best overall

Lightweight and fast with a stunning OLED display

The 2023 Acer Swift Edge comes with AMD Ryzen 7000 series processors and powerful Nvidia RTX 4000-series graphics cards. Additionally, it packs a beautiful OLED display with a 3.2K resolution and a 120Hz refresh rate.

$1300 at Best Buy $1600 at B&H

If you want a laptop that's geared specifically towards gaming, then the Alienware m16 is the one for you. While it may be quite expensive, this powerful laptop can easily handle any modern triple-A game you throw at it, and its high refresh rate display further enhances your gaming experience.

Alternatively, you should go for the MSI Modern 15 (2023) if you don't want to spend a fortune on a laptop. Despite its 720p camera and lack of support for DDR5 memory, it offers solid performance for a budget laptop and can last you quite a while if you plan to use it for everyday tasks.
item.description:
Typically, Intel processors have been much more prevalent than AMD ones, especially in laptops. Even today, many of the best laptops you can buy are powered by Intel processors. However, since introducing the Ryzen family, AMD has made great strides in becoming more popular, and more and more fantastic laptops have shown up with AMD Ryzen processors inside.

These days, you can find really great laptops powered by AMD Ryzen in all different segments of the market. Whether you want a new gaming rig, a premium laptop, or something to get work done, AMD processors are now at the heart of many devices, and they're some of the best you can find, too. If you're looking to upgrade to a new AMD laptop, we've rounded up the best options you can find today. As we've mentioned, these laptops cover a lot of ground, so no matter what you're looking for, you're bound to find something you like here.

  • Acer Swift Edge 16 (2023)
    Best overall
    $1300 at Best Buy
  • Alienware m16 (AMD)
    Best gaming laptop
    $1700 at Dell
  • MSI Bravo 15
    Best budget gaming laptop
    $923 at Walmart
  • Dell Inspiron 14 2-in-1
    Best budget convertible
    $550 at Dell (AMD)
  • Lenovo Yoga 7 (16-inch, 2023)
    Best convertible
    $640 at Lenovo
  • Source: Amazon
    ASUS Vivobook Pro 15
    Best laptops for creators
    $1400 at Amazon
  • HP Envy x360 15.6 (2023)
    Best business laptop
    $800 at Best Buy
  • MSI Modern 15
    Best budget
    $700 at Amazon

The top AMD Ryzen laptops in 2023

Acer Swift Edge 16 (2023)
Best overall

Lightweight and fast with a stunning OLED display

The 2023 Acer Swift Edge comes with AMD Ryzen 7000 series processors and powerful Nvidia RTX 4000-series graphics cards. Additionally, it packs a beautiful OLED display with a 3.2K resolution and a 120Hz refresh rate.

Pros
  • Fast AMD Ryzen 7 7040 processors
  • Stunning 120Hz OLED display
  • Extremely thin and light for its size
Cons
  • Lack of true 4K resolution
$1300 at Best Buy $1600 at B&H

The Acer Swift Edge was already one of our favorite thin and lightweight laptops, and the 2023 model improves upon its predecessor's design in almost every way except the display resolution.

Performance-wise, it's packing an AMD Ryzen 7 7840U processor with 8 cores and 16 threads for excellent performance. This processor also includes Radeon 780M integrated graphics for solid performance in 3D workloads. Plus, 16GB of LPDDR5 RAM and up to a 2TB SSD round out a top-tier set of specs.

The laptop has a 16-inch OLED panel with a refresh rate of 120Hz, making it incredibly smooth and vibrant. Above that display is a 1440p webcam, which is always great to see. However, the laptop has a 3200x2000 display resolution, which is lower than the 4K resolution offered by its predecessor, though still more than sharp enough for this size.

Despite the powerful processor and fantastic display, the Acer Swift Edge 16 is extremely light for a 16-inch laptop, coming in at a mere 2.7 pounds thanks to the magnesium alloy chassis. Even with this thin and lightweight design, you get a lot of ports, including two USB Type-C, two USB Type-A, an HDMI, and a headphone jack. This makes for a well-rounded setup, especially considering the device's thinness. You should be able to use most of your peripherals with it without needing adapters, which is excellent. Acer really knocked it out of the park with the Swift Edge 16, and it's one of the best laptops you can buy in general, but especially if you want an AMD Ryzen processor.

Alienware m16 (AMD)
Best gaming laptop

Extreme performance for demanding games

The Alienware m16 is a super-powerful gaming laptop packing AMD Ryzen 7045HX series processors and Nvidia GeForce RTX 40 series GPUs. It also has a large 16-inch screen with a 16:10 aspect ratio, great for gaming and content creation.

Pros
  • Top-tier AMD Ryzen CPUs and Nvidia GeForce GPUs
  • Tall 16:10 display is great for gaming and content creation
  • Sleek Alienware design language
Cons
  • Very thick and heavy
  • Expensive
$1700 at Dell $2800 at Best Buy

AMD processors have always been more popular in gaming laptops, and if that's what you're looking for, the Alienware m16 is the laptop for you. This is one of the fastest laptops on the market, starting with powerful AMD Ryzen 7045HX processors, up to a Ryzen 9 7945HX with 12 cores and 24 threads. If that's not wild enough, the laptop also comes with up to an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4080 (a 4090 model will come later) GPU, and these two components can use up to 240W of power combined. Rounding things out, you can go up to 64GB of RAM and a massive 8.5TB of storage.

The 16-inch display comes in a 16:10 aspect ratio, making this a super laptop for gaming and productivity. The base model has Quad HD+ resolution and a 165Hz refresh rate, but you can upgrade to a 240Hz panel that also covers 100% of DCI-P3 for nicer colors overall. If you value frame rates more, there's also a Full HD+ 480Hz panel for maximum smoothness in esports titles. Above the display is a 1080p webcam with optional Windows Hello support, depending on your chosen display.

The Alienware m16 features the company's signature design language, with an all-black chassis and RGB lighting on the keyboard and around the exhaust vents on the back. Of course, given the powerful specs, it's fairly thick and heavy, going up to 7.28 pounds depending on the specs you choose. As usual with gaming laptops, ports are plentiful, with two USB Type-C and two Type-A ports, HDMI, Mini DisplayPort, RJ45 Ethernet (2.5Gbps), a headphone jack, and a full-size SD card reader.

You'll have to pay a pretty penny for the privilege of owning the Alienware m16, but it's a powerful laptop that won't let you down if you want to run the latest games.

MSI Bravo 15
Best budget gaming laptop

Solid gaming performance at a lower price

Armed with Nvidia's 40-series GPUs and AMD's Ryzen 7035 processors, the MSI Bravo 15 is a fantastic lineup of gaming laptops that's also light on your wallet. What's more, this budget gaming laptop packs an FHD display with a smooth 144Hz refresh rate.

Pros
  • Fast AMD 7035 processors and Nvidia 40 series GPUs
  • Smooth 144Hz refresh rate
  • Inexpensive for a gaming laptop
Cons
  • 720p webcam
  • Bulky
$923 at Walmart $1000 at Best Buy

AMD CPUs are known to deliver powerful performance at a low price, and AMD's Ryzen 7000 laptop processors are no exception. So, if you're looking for a cheap laptop that can play the latest games at high frame rates, look no further than the MSI Bravo 15, powered by these heavy-hitting CPUs.

From its specifications alone, it's clear that the MSI Bravo 15 is no slouch. The base model comes with a Ryzen 5 7535HS processor and is powered by Nvidia's RTX 4050 GPU, all while costing $800. If you want more performance, you can go for the pricier variant with a Ryzen 7 7735HS CPU and an RTX 4060. Both MSI Bravo 15 models have a 512GB PCIe NVMe SSD and offer a buttery smooth FHD 144Hz display for all your high-speed gaming needs. Factor in the blazing-fast 8GB DDR5 4800MHz memory, and these laptops can handle most modern games with ease.

One downside of this laptop is its bulky design. It weighs almost 5 pounds and has a 24.8-millimeter thickness, meaning it's not the most portable gaming laptop on the market. As for the ports, the MSI Bravo 15 houses all essential connections, including a USB Type-C connector, three USB Type-A ports, and one HDMI connector. It even comes with a headphone/microphone combo jack.

Unlike most gaming laptops, the MSI Bravo doesn't feature flashy aesthetics. Instead, the laptop sports a more professional look, making it great for users who want a sleek and powerful laptop for everyday work. While the MSI Bravo 15 may not be the very best laptop if you're a heavy gamer, it's a solid choice for a budget gaming device.

Dell Inspiron 14 2-in-1
Best budget convertible

That's it for this list! You really can't go wrong with any of these laptops, but if we had to pick the best one from the bunch, it'd certainly be the Acer Swift Edge 16. It is extremely light, yet it has a large and sharp 4K display with stunning OLED technology. The high-performance processor on the Acer Swift Edge 16 is just the icing on the cake. You can't ask for much more here, even if the processor inside isn't the newest around anymore.

Acer Swift Edge 16 (2023)
Best overall

Lightweight and fast with a stunning OLED display

The 2023 Acer Swift Edge comes with AMD Ryzen 7000 series processors and powerful Nvidia RTX 4000-series graphics cards. Additionally, it packs a beautiful OLED display with a 3.2K resolution and a 120Hz refresh rate.

$1300 at Best Buy $1600 at B&H

If you want a laptop that's geared specifically towards gaming, then the Alienware m16 is the one for you. While it may be quite expensive, this powerful laptop can easily handle any modern triple-A game you throw at it, and its high refresh rate display further enhances your gaming experience.

Alternatively, you should go for the MSI Modern 15 (2023) if you don't want to spend a fortune on a laptop. Despite its 720p camera and lack of support for DDR5 memory, it offers solid performance for a budget laptop and can last you quite a while if you plan to use it for everyday tasks.
item.content:encoded:
Typically, Intel processors have been much more prevalent than AMD ones, especially in laptops. Even today, many of the best laptops you can buy are powered by Intel processors. However, since introducing the Ryzen family, AMD has made great strides in becoming more popular, and more and more fantastic laptops have shown up with AMD Ryzen processors inside.

These days, you can find really great laptops powered by AMD Ryzen in all different segments of the market. Whether you want a new gaming rig, a premium laptop, or something to get work done, AMD processors are now at the heart of many devices, and they're some of the best you can find, too. If you're looking to upgrade to a new AMD laptop, we've rounded up the best options you can find today. As we've mentioned, these laptops cover a lot of ground, so no matter what you're looking for, you're bound to find something you like here.

  • Acer Swift Edge 16 (2023)
    Best overall
    $1300 at Best Buy
  • Alienware m16 (AMD)
    Best gaming laptop
    $1700 at Dell
  • MSI Bravo 15
    Best budget gaming laptop
    $923 at Walmart
  • Dell Inspiron 14 2-in-1
    Best budget convertible
    $550 at Dell (AMD)
  • Lenovo Yoga 7 (16-inch, 2023)
    Best convertible
    $640 at Lenovo
  • Source: Amazon
    ASUS Vivobook Pro 15
    Best laptops for creators
    $1400 at Amazon
  • HP Envy x360 15.6 (2023)
    Best business laptop
    $800 at Best Buy
  • MSI Modern 15
    Best budget
    $700 at Amazon

The top AMD Ryzen laptops in 2023

Acer Swift Edge 16 (2023)
Best overall

Lightweight and fast with a stunning OLED display

The 2023 Acer Swift Edge comes with AMD Ryzen 7000 series processors and powerful Nvidia RTX 4000-series graphics cards. Additionally, it packs a beautiful OLED display with a 3.2K resolution and a 120Hz refresh rate.

Pros
  • Fast AMD Ryzen 7 7040 processors
  • Stunning 120Hz OLED display
  • Extremely thin and light for its size
Cons
  • Lack of true 4K resolution
$1300 at Best Buy $1600 at B&H

The Acer Swift Edge was already one of our favorite thin and lightweight laptops, and the 2023 model improves upon its predecessor's design in almost every way except the display resolution.

Performance-wise, it's packing an AMD Ryzen 7 7840U processor with 8 cores and 16 threads for excellent performance. This processor also includes Radeon 780M integrated graphics for solid performance in 3D workloads. Plus, 16GB of LPDDR5 RAM and up to a 2TB SSD round out a top-tier set of specs.

The laptop has a 16-inch OLED panel with a refresh rate of 120Hz, making it incredibly smooth and vibrant. Above that display is a 1440p webcam, which is always great to see. However, the laptop has a 3200x2000 display resolution, which is lower than the 4K resolution offered by its predecessor, though still more than sharp enough for this size.

Despite the powerful processor and fantastic display, the Acer Swift Edge 16 is extremely light for a 16-inch laptop, coming in at a mere 2.7 pounds thanks to the magnesium alloy chassis. Even with this thin and lightweight design, you get a lot of ports, including two USB Type-C, two USB Type-A, an HDMI, and a headphone jack. This makes for a well-rounded setup, especially considering the device's thinness. You should be able to use most of your peripherals with it without needing adapters, which is excellent. Acer really knocked it out of the park with the Swift Edge 16, and it's one of the best laptops you can buy in general, but especially if you want an AMD Ryzen processor.

Alienware m16 (AMD)
Best gaming laptop

Extreme performance for demanding games

The Alienware m16 is a super-powerful gaming laptop packing AMD Ryzen 7045HX series processors and Nvidia GeForce RTX 40 series GPUs. It also has a large 16-inch screen with a 16:10 aspect ratio, great for gaming and content creation.

Pros
  • Top-tier AMD Ryzen CPUs and Nvidia GeForce GPUs
  • Tall 16:10 display is great for gaming and content creation
  • Sleek Alienware design language
Cons
  • Very thick and heavy
  • Expensive
$1700 at Dell $2800 at Best Buy

AMD processors have always been more popular in gaming laptops, and if that's what you're looking for, the Alienware m16 is the laptop for you. This is one of the fastest laptops on the market, starting with powerful AMD Ryzen 7045HX processors, up to a Ryzen 9 7945HX with 12 cores and 24 threads. If that's not wild enough, the laptop also comes with up to an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4080 (a 4090 model will come later) GPU, and these two components can use up to 240W of power combined. Rounding things out, you can go up to 64GB of RAM and a massive 8.5TB of storage.

The 16-inch display comes in a 16:10 aspect ratio, making this a super laptop for gaming and productivity. The base model has Quad HD+ resolution and a 165Hz refresh rate, but you can upgrade to a 240Hz panel that also covers 100% of DCI-P3 for nicer colors overall. If you value frame rates more, there's also a Full HD+ 480Hz panel for maximum smoothness in esports titles. Above the display is a 1080p webcam with optional Windows Hello support, depending on your chosen display.

The Alienware m16 features the company's signature design language, with an all-black chassis and RGB lighting on the keyboard and around the exhaust vents on the back. Of course, given the powerful specs, it's fairly thick and heavy, going up to 7.28 pounds depending on the specs you choose. As usual with gaming laptops, ports are plentiful, with two USB Type-C and two Type-A ports, HDMI, Mini DisplayPort, RJ45 Ethernet (2.5Gbps), a headphone jack, and a full-size SD card reader.

You'll have to pay a pretty penny for the privilege of owning the Alienware m16, but it's a powerful laptop that won't let you down if you want to run the latest games.

MSI Bravo 15
Best budget gaming laptop

Solid gaming performance at a lower price

Armed with Nvidia's 40-series GPUs and AMD's Ryzen 7035 processors, the MSI Bravo 15 is a fantastic lineup of gaming laptops that's also light on your wallet. What's more, this budget gaming laptop packs an FHD display with a smooth 144Hz refresh rate.

Pros
  • Fast AMD 7035 processors and Nvidia 40 series GPUs
  • Smooth 144Hz refresh rate
  • Inexpensive for a gaming laptop
Cons
  • 720p webcam
  • Bulky
$923 at Walmart $1000 at Best Buy

AMD CPUs are known to deliver powerful performance at a low price, and AMD's Ryzen 7000 laptop processors are no exception. So, if you're looking for a cheap laptop that can play the latest games at high frame rates, look no further than the MSI Bravo 15, powered by these heavy-hitting CPUs.

From its specifications alone, it's clear that the MSI Bravo 15 is no slouch. The base model comes with a Ryzen 5 7535HS processor and is powered by Nvidia's RTX 4050 GPU, all while costing $800. If you want more performance, you can go for the pricier variant with a Ryzen 7 7735HS CPU and an RTX 4060. Both MSI Bravo 15 models have a 512GB PCIe NVMe SSD and offer a buttery smooth FHD 144Hz display for all your high-speed gaming needs. Factor in the blazing-fast 8GB DDR5 4800MHz memory, and these laptops can handle most modern games with ease.

One downside of this laptop is its bulky design. It weighs almost 5 pounds and has a 24.8-millimeter thickness, meaning it's not the most portable gaming laptop on the market. As for the ports, the MSI Bravo 15 houses all essential connections, including a USB Type-C connector, three USB Type-A ports, and one HDMI connector. It even comes with a headphone/microphone combo jack.

Unlike most gaming laptops, the MSI Bravo doesn't feature flashy aesthetics. Instead, the laptop sports a more professional look, making it great for users who want a sleek and powerful laptop for everyday work. While the MSI Bravo 15 may not be the very best laptop if you're a heavy gamer, it's a solid choice for a budget gaming device.

Dell Inspiron 14 2-in-1
Best budget convertible

Great performance for a lower price

A great convertible for those on a budget, the Dell Inspiron 14 is a mainstream 2-in-1 laptop that offers solid performance thanks to its AMD Ryzen 7030 processors. It also has a sleek build, a 1080p webcam, and a tall 16:10 display.

Pros
  • Fast and energy-efficient AMD Ryzen 7030 series processors
  • Solid display with a 1080p webcam
  • Decent port selection
Cons
  • No support for DDR5 memory
  • Low peak brightness of 250 nits
$550 at Dell (AMD) $700 at Best Buy (AMD)

If you want the versatility of a convertible without breaking the bank, Dell's Inspiron 14 is the one for you. We've reviewed multiple iterations of this budget 2-in-1 laptop in the past and loved every single one. Fortunately, the 2023 model is even better thanks to some performance improvements in the CPU department.

The latest Inspiron 14 laptop ships with AMD's Ryzen 7030 processors, and you can get up to a Ryzen 7 7730U with 8 cores and 16 threads. It seems like a minor upgrade at first, but these processors are more efficient and faster than the previous 2022 and 2021 models. While the lack of support for DDR5 memory can be a downer, the 16GB of LPDDR4X will suffice for most users. You also won't have to worry about running out of space as you can configure it up to 1TB of SSD space.

As for the display, you're looking at a 14-inch WVA panel with a 1920 x 1200 screen resolution and a 60Hz refresh rate. The touchscreen display has a tall 16:10 aspect ratio, making the Inspiron 14 a fantastic budget option for productivity-related tasks. At a peak brightness of 250 nits, it's not good enough to be used outdoors. Fortunately, you do get a 1080p front camera, which isn't something you'll observe in a laptop in this price range.

The Inspiron 14 rocks a sleek silver body and weighs 3.48 pounds, so it's not the lightest convertible on the market. With two USB Type-C ports, one USB Type-A connection, an SD card slot, an audio jack, and an HDMI port, the convertible provides decent connectivity for the average user.

All in all, the Dell Inspiron 14 is certainly one of the best laptops you can find with an AMD Ryzen processor. It's not aimed at the high-end of the market, but it's fantastic for just about everyone else.

Lenovo Yoga 7 (16-inch, 2023)
Best convertible

A versatile form factor with high-performance specs

The 2023 Lenovo Yoga 7 is a powerful convertible laptop with AMD Ryzen 7035 series processors, delivering strong performance in a versatile form factor. The 16-inch display comes in Full HD+ resolution, perfect for getting work done.

Pros
  • AMD Ryzen 7035 processors deliver strong performance
  • Large 16-inch display is great for multi-tasking
  • Plenty of ports
Cons
  • The display isn't very vibrant
  • Somewhat heavy
$640 at Lenovo $750 at Best Buy

If you want a laptop that's just a bit more versatile, the Lenovo Yoga 7 is a fantastic convertible laptop with high-end AMD Ryzen processors and a big canvas to work on.

Starting with performance, the Lenovo Yoga 7 (2023) is powered by AMD Ryzen 7035U series processors, delivering strong performance with up to 8 cores and 16 threads, plus Radeon 680M integrated graphics. With this GPU, it's actually possible to play a lot of games as long as you can tweak some settings. It can also be configured with up to 16GB of RAM and a 1TB SSD for a great experience all around.

The display isn't as impressive as the Acer Swift Edge, but it's still a large 16-inch panel with a 16:10 aspect ratio, so it's great for productivity. It comes in Full HD+ resolution, though you may find it disappointing that it only covers 45% of NTSC, so colors won't be super vibrant. Being a convertible means the screen supports touch and pen input, too. Above the display is a 1080p webcam with Windows Hello support, which is always nice to see.

In terms of design, the Lenovo Yoga 7 looks nice with its curved edges, especially in the Tidal Teal colorway, but the Storm Grey model is more widely available and a bit more boring. At 4.49 pounds, it's a bit on the heavier side, though it makes sense for an aluminum laptop. You do get a ton of ports, though, including two USB Type-C and two Type-A ports, HDMI, a headphone jack, and a microSD card reader. Unfortunately, the USB Type-C ports don't support USB4.

It's not quite as premium as some Intel-powered convertibles, but the Lenovo Yoga 7 is a great machine at a very reasonable price for what it offers.

Source: Amazon
ASUS Vivobook Pro 15
Best laptops for creators

Blazing-fast performance for creative professionals

Designed for creators, the ASUS Vivobook Pro 15 is a powerful yet affordable 15.6-inch laptop that offers tons of features for those into content creation. With up to a Ryzen 9 7940HS CPU and an RTX 4060 graphics card, you won't have to worry about running into performance issues either.

Pros
  • Ryzen 7000-series CPUs can crush most creative workloads
  • RTX 4000 GPUs are great for video editing and graphics rendering
  • 1080p webcam with privacy shutter
  • Better security with a fingerprint sensor built into the power key
Cons
  • Lack of an OLED panel can be a deal-breaker
  • 16:9 aspect is a bit lacking for productivity tasks
$1400 at Amazon $1400 at Best Buy

Looking for a sleek and powerful laptop for your creativity needs? The ASUS Vivobook Pro 15 is definitely the one you should get.

From a performance standpoint, the latest Vivobook Pro 15 laptop supports up to a Ryzen 9 7940HS processor, which provides unmatched performance with its 8 cores, 16 threads, and 5.2GHz boost clock. On the GPU front, you're looking at a highly capable RTX 4060 that can handle most video editing and rendering tasks with ease. You also get up to 32GB of LPDDR5X memory alongside a 1TB SSD for an all-round solid experience.

While the 15.6-inch FHD+ display isn't bad per se, it can be a bit lacking considering most Intel-based laptops in the Vivobook series feature OLED displays. Similarly, the 16:9 aspect ratio isn't very optimized for creators. Nevertheless, ASUS somewhat compensates for these drawbacks with a 144Hz refresh rate and an 84% screen-to-body ratio. As for the webcam, you're looking at a 1080p front camera with a built-in privacy shutter.

The Vivobook Pro 15 bears a robust, professional-looking chassis with a 180° hinge. At 3.97 pounds, it is a bit on the bulkier side, though that is to be expected considering its powerful hardware. Its backlit chiclet keyboard also has antimicrobial properties, and you even get a fingerprint sensor integrated into the power button for enhanced security. Finally, the laptop includes three USB Type-A ports, one USB Type-C port, an HDMI 2.1 connection, a 3.5mm audio jack, and an SD card reader, so it does cover most needs you might have.

Leaving aside its display limitations, the Vivobook Pro 15 is a good laptop in almost all respects, and it's hard to fault it considering its price point.

HP Envy x360 15.6 (2023)
Best business laptop

Top-tier performance and a sleek professional design

Compact and budget-friendly, the HP Envy x360 comes with AMD Ryzen 7030 series processors. It has a touchscreen display that supports pen input, and you can outfit this high-end business laptop with an OLED on HP's website.

Pros
  • Solid performance thanks to AMD Ryzen 7030 series processors
  • Sleek and modern design
  • Touchscreen support
Cons
  • Last-gen LPDDR4X memory
  • Rather bulky
$950 at HP (AMD) $800 at Best Buy

The HP Envy lineup has served the needs of professionals for more than a decade, and the 2023 is a worthy addition to the series.

Powering this high-end business laptop are AMD Ryzen 7030 series processors, up to a Ryzen 7 7730U processor with 8 cores and 16 threads. These processors deliver fantastic performance, and they feature integrated Radeon RX Vega 8 graphics, which deliver strong GPU performance for basic tasks. Since the LPDR4X RAM modules are soldered onto the motherboard, you can't upgrade them, though 16GB of memory should be enough for most business-oriented tasks. You also get up to 1TB worth of SSD storage.

The HP Envy x360 has a 15.6-inch display with a 16:9 aspect ratio, but it gives you a couple of configuration options. The base model comes in Full HD+ resolution (1920 x 1080) with touchscreen support, but you can upgrade to an OLED panel for a rich viewing experience. This panel is sharper and has much more vibrant colors thanks to OLED technology. HP has also equipped the laptop's 5MP HD webcam with IR sensors, so it's compatible with Windows Hello facial recognition.

Design-wise, the 2023 model of the Envy x360 follows the same formula of a compact and sleek-looking chassis as the last-gen models. At 4.03 pounds, it's on the bulkier side for a business laptop. It also has a decent collection of ports as you get two USB Type-C connections, one USB Type-A port, an HDMI connection, an SD card reader, and a headphone/microphone combo jack.

With its affordable price, the HP Envy x360 is easily the best business laptop with Team Red processors.

MSI Modern 15
Best budget

Solid specs at an affordable price

The MSI Modern 15 is a great budget laptop that's powered by AMD's Ryzen 7030 U-series processors and feature a large 15.6-inch Full HD display. Measuring 19.8 millimeters in thickness, it's also relatively compact, though it does weigh 5.6 pounds.

Pros
  • AMD's Ryzen 7030U processors offer solid performance
  • Plenty of ports
  • Affordable
Cons
  • 720p webcam
  • Doesn't support DDR5 memory
$700 at Amazon $700 at Newegg $700 at Walmart

If the rest of the laptops on this list seem a bit pricey, then you should consider checking out the MSI Modern 15. Its base model comes with the 6-core AMD Ryzen 7530U processor that has base and turbo clock speeds of 2.00GHz and 4.50GHz, respectively. If that sounds underwhelming, you can go for the slightly pricier variant, which offers the better-performing Ryzen 7730U CPU. While the lack of support for DDR5 memory can be seen as a drawback, the laptop's 16GB DDR4 RAM works well for day-to-day tasks, and its 1920x1080 display resolution and 16:9 aspect ratio are good enough for the price. You're also covered on the storage front as the laptop includes a fast 1TB NVMe SSD that you won't need to upgrade for a long time.

Weighing around 3.85 pounds, the MSI Modern 15 certainly isn't as light as the HP Pavilion Aero 13 (2023), but it's not as heavy as a typical budget laptop. As for the ports, the laptop has one HDMI slot, a headphone jack, one USB Type-C connection, and three USB Type-A ports.

Its webcam, however, isn't particularly great. The 720p sensor on the webcam can cause poor video quality, so you're better off spending some cash on a dedicated webcam instead of using the laptop's built-in camera. But considering that the MSI Modern 15 starts at $700, it's hard not to recommend this budget-friendly laptop.

Best AMD Ryzen laptops in 2023: The bottom line

That's it for this list! You really can't go wrong with any of these laptops, but if we had to pick the best one from the bunch, it'd certainly be the Acer Swift Edge 16. It is extremely light, yet it has a large and sharp 4K display with stunning OLED technology. The high-performance processor on the Acer Swift Edge 16 is just the icing on the cake. You can't ask for much more here, even if the processor inside isn't the newest around anymore.

Acer Swift Edge 16 (2023)
Best overall

Lightweight and fast with a stunning OLED display

The 2023 Acer Swift Edge comes with AMD Ryzen 7000 series processors and powerful Nvidia RTX 4000-series graphics cards. Additionally, it packs a beautiful OLED display with a 3.2K resolution and a 120Hz refresh rate.

$1300 at Best Buy $1600 at B&H

If you want a laptop that's geared specifically towards gaming, then the Alienware m16 is the one for you. While it may be quite expensive, this powerful laptop can easily handle any modern triple-A game you throw at it, and its high refresh rate display further enhances your gaming experience.

Alternatively, you should go for the MSI Modern 15 (2023) if you don't want to spend a fortune on a laptop. Despite its 720p camera and lack of support for DDR5 memory, it offers solid performance for a budget laptop and can last you quite a while if you plan to use it for everyday tasks.

item.title: Best silent PC mouse in 2023
item.itunes:title: Best silent PC mouse in 2023
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
The constant clicking and scrolling sounds from a mouse may not be a big distraction for most, but they can be highly undesirable in many situations. Consider a scenario where you need to work overnight on an important project but don’t want to wake up your roommates or family members. A silent mouse is infinitely useful in this case as it can help you engage in productive tasks without worrying about disturbing your surroundings. Moreover, switching to a mouse with noise-reduction capabilities is beneficial if you prefer a noise-free working environment.

With a variety of quiet pointers available on the market, it might be difficult to pick the perfect mouse for your computing needs. To help you with your search, we've compiled a list of the best silent mice that make zero noise without sacrificing functionality or comfort.

  • Razer Pro Click Mini
    Editor's choice
    $78 at Amazon
  • Logitech M330 Silent Plus Wireless Mouse
    Runner-up
    $21 at Newegg
  • Logitech MX Master 3S
    Premium pick
    $93 at Best Buy
  • Source: Amazon
    Ugreen Wireless Mouse
    Best wireless
    $18 at Walmart
  • Source: Amazon
    HP 280 Silent Mouse
    Budget pick
    $18 at Amazon
  • Source: Amazon
    MOJO Pro Performance Silent Gaming Mouse
    Best gaming
    $55 at Amazon
  • Lenovo 600 Bluetooth Silent Mouse
    Best flat
    $21 at Newegg
  • Source: Amazon
    Logitech Lift Vertical Mouse
    Best left-handed mouse
    $68 at Amazon

Maintain a noise-free workspace with our top silent PC mouse picks

Razer Pro Click Mini
Editor's choice

The all-rounder silent mouse

The Razer Pro Click Mini is a compact productivity mouse with silent switches and a sleek design.  The mouse uses silent mechanical switches to eliminate clicking noises. Its multi-device functionality allows you to control up to four systems at a time without pairing and unpairing a PC every time you need to cycle between your systems.

Weight
198 g
Sensor
Razer 5G Advanced Optical Sensor
Battery
~725 hours on Bluetooth, ~465 hours with dongle
Connectivity
Bluetooth, 2.4GHz dongle
Maximum DPI
12000
Pros
  • Supports horizontal scrolling
  • High DPI limit
  • Professional, minimalist design
Cons
  • Can be a little too small
$78 at Amazon $80 at Best Buy

The Razer Pro Click Mini marks a departure from Razer’s typical RGB aesthetics with its all-white design. Despite being comfortable for daily usage, it’s extremely robust with its switch lifespan of 15 million clicks. Its HyperScroll Technology allows you to switch between fast, precision, and horizontal scrolling modes on the fly. The mouse connects wirelessly to your system via Bluetooth or a receiver, and its high sensitivity of 12,000 DPI and seven programmable buttons make it a solid choice for a wireless gaming mouse.

Logitech M330 Silent Plus Wireless Mouse
Runner-up

A solid quiet mouse

The Logitech M330 Silent Plus is a quiet mouse that offers high-precision tracking while retaining a portable design. It features advanced optical tracking which allows it to maintain high accuracy up to 33ft and is comfortable to use thanks to its soft rubber side grips.

Weight
91 g
Connectivity
USB dongle
Battery Life
~24 months
DPI
1000
Sensor
Logitech High Precision Optical Tracking
Pros
  • Supports most OS
  • Long battery life
  • Portable
Cons
  • Doesn't support Bluetooth connectivity
  • Low DPI
$28 at Amazon $21 at Newegg

Featuring Logitech’s SilentTouch Technology that reduces 90% of its clicking and scrolling sounds, the Logitech M330 Silent Plus is the perfect companion for a night owl. It can be used wirelessly by plugging a mini-USB receiver into your system and is compatible with all major operating systems, including Windows, MacOS, Linux, and ChromeOS. The mouse automatically switches to sleep mode when not in use to conserve power and its battery can easily last for 24 months under normal usage.

Logitech MX Master 3S
Premium pick

The best-performing silent mouse, for a price

$93 $100 Save $7

The MX Master 3S is an excellent mouse if you want to amp up your productivity. It offers multiple programmable buttons, Logitech's MagSpeed scroll wheel, multi-device support, an ergonomic design, and more.

Weight
141 g
Sensor
Darkfield high-precision sensor
Battery
~70 days
Connectivity
Bluetooth, Wireless dongle
Maximum DPI
8000
Pros
  • Seven programmable buttons
  • Can work on any surface
  • MagSpeed Scroll wheel supports free and ratchet scrolling
Cons
  • Expensive
$100 at Amazon $93 at Best Buy $100 at Newegg

While it may be on the pricier side, the Logitech MX Master 3S is our top contender for the best premium silent PC mouse thanks to its vast set of features. It can easily connect with your system via Bluetooth or a Logi bolt USB receiver and its multi-device support allows it to work with 3 devices at once. Despite offering similar noise reduction capabilities to the MX Anywhere 3S, the MX Master 3S has more side buttons and a more ergonomic design. Another feature of the Logitech MX Master 3S worth pointing out is its MagSpeed Scroll Wheel which allows you to switch between free scrolling and ratchet scrolling modes as you see fit.

Source: Amazon
Ugreen Wireless Mouse
Best wireless

A quiet mouse that doesn't bog you down with wires

If you want a quiet mouse that lets you operate your PC from a distance, the Ugreen Wireless Mouse is for you. With support for Bluetooth 5.0 and wireless 2.4GHz dongle, this cordless mouse is compatible with a wide range of devices.

Weight
79 g
Sensor
Optical sensor
Battery
~18 months
Connectivity
2.4Ghz USB dongle, Bluetooth 5.0
Maximum DPI
4000
Pros
  • Supports both Bluetooth and wireless dongle
  • Inexpensive
Cons
  • Not the best mouse for gamers
$23 at Amazon $18 at Walmart

True to its name, the Ugreen Wireless Mouse frees you from the confines of cables by allowing you to pair it with your PC via Bluetooth or the included wireless dongle. In addition to two extra mappable buttons on its side, this silent mouse has excellent tracing properties thanks to its optical sensor. You also get multiple sensitivity levels at 1000, 1600, 2000, and 4000 DPI to adjust the pointer speed according to your accuracy requirements.

Source: Amazon
HP 280 Silent Mouse
Budget pick

Budget-friendly silent mouse

$18 $25 Save $7

The HP 280 Silent Wireless Mouse comes in a comfortable full-size design, and it features silent switches, so you can get work done without bothering the people around you. Sure, the HP 280 isn't the fanciest mouse, but it's a solid basic option.

Weight
95.3 g
Sensor
Multi-surface optical sensor
Battery
~18 months
Connectivity
2.4GHz wireless dongle
Pros
  • Affordable
  • Works on any surface
  • Reliable connection up to 32 feet
Cons
  • Lack of Bluetooth connectivity
  • Fixed sensitivity of 1600 DPI
$18 at Amazon $25 at HP

The HP 280 Silent Mouse may not have extra buttons, RGB lighting, or the other bells and whistles of a premium mouse, but it's a great option if you want a budget pointer that can get the job done. Thanks to its multi-sensor optical scanner, HP's budget mouse can precisely track your movements without a mouse pad. On top of that, you can keep the mouse at a distance of 32.8 feet without experiencing any connectivity issues whatsoever.

Source: Amazon
MOJO Pro Performance Silent Gaming Mouse
Best gaming

Our go-to mouse for silent gaming

The MOJO Pro Performance Silent Gaming Mouse is a highly customizable pointer that forgoes wireless connectivity in favor of raw performance.  Its advanced PMW3336 sensor allows it to hit 12,000 DPI and 1000Hz in polling rate, and its nine customizable buttons can be mapped to the keys of your choice.
 

Weight
127 g
Sensor
PMW3336 Gaming Sensor
Connectivity
Wired
Maximum DPI
12000
Pros
  • Highly customizable
  • Force-adjustable buttons
Cons
  • Lack of wireless connectivity
$55 at Amazon $60 at Walmart

Despite lacking wireless connectivity, the MOJO Pro Performance Silent Gaming Mouse provides more than enough features to justify its position as our favorite silent gaming mouse. The mouse is highly customizable, allowing you to tweak its sensitivity, sensor height, RGB lighting, and button mappings. You can also use the software available on MOJO's websites to save your mouse’s configurations in the form of mouse profiles. In addition to its high 12000 DPI and 1000 Hz polling rate, the MOJO Pro Performance mouse also features force-adjustable keys, and you can change the amount of pressure applied to the left and right mouse buttons.

Lenovo 600 Bluetooth Silent Mouse
Best flat

The most portable silent mouse

The Lenovo 600 Bluetooth Silent Mouse is a solid flat mouse with a compact and ambidextrous design. This lightweight mouse connects to your PC via Bluetooth 5.0 and supports Microsoft’s Swift Key feature to instantly pair up with your system.

Weight
61 g
Sensor
Lenovo Blue Optical Sensor
Battery
~12 months
Connectivity
Bluetooth 5.0
Maximum DPI
2400
Pros
  • Ambidextrous design
  • Compatible with Microsoft's Swift Key facility
  • Highly portable
Cons
  • A tad too small
$25 at Amazon $21 at Newegg

If you’re on the lookout for a flat, slim silent mouse, then the Lenovo 600 Bluetooth Silent Mouse is your best bet. In addition to its ambidextrous design, the mouse also provides a dedicated DPI switch allowing you to alternate between three sensitivity levels of 800, 1600, and 2400 DPI. Its small form factor combined with the ability to work on any surface, including glass and mirror, makes it the perfect silent mouse for on-the-go productivity.

Source: Amazon
Logitech Lift Vertical Mouse
Best left-handed mouse

For left-handed users who prefer ergonomic design

The Logitech Lift is a quiet mouse designed to eliminate wrist strain with its vertical orientation. This ergonomic mouse can hit a maximum sensitivity of 4000 DPI and includes two extra buttons to assist in your work.

Weight
125 g
Sensor
Logitech Advanced Optical Tracking
Battery
~24 months
Connectivity
Logi Bolt USB receiver, Bluetooth
Maximum DPI
4000
Pros
  • Easy to set up
  • Comfortable to use
Cons
  • Expensive
  • A bit on the heavier side
$68 at Amazon $70 at Newegg

The Logitech Lift Vertical Mouse's specialty lies in its ergonomic design that improves your hand posture and reduces hand fatigue, all while ensuring noise-free usage. This left-handed mouse is also compatible with the Logi Bolt technology, so you can use a single dongle to control it in tandem with other Logitech accessories. You're also covered on the sensitivity front as the mouse has a variable 400-4000 DPI range.

Best silent PC mouse in 2023: Summary

And that's it for this list! While you can't go wrong with any of these pointers, you should choose Razer’s Pro Click Mini as it offers the best bang for the buck. Its quality of life features such as horizontal scrolling and multi-device support, paired with a simple, minimalist design make it perfect for use in a professional environment.

Razer Pro Click Mini
Editor's choice

The all-rounder silent mouse

The Razer Pro Click Mini is a compact productivity mouse with silent switches and a sleek design.  The mouse uses silent mechanical switches to eliminate clicking noises. Its multi-device functionality allows you to control up to four systems at a time without pairing and unpairing a PC every time you need to cycle between your systems.

$78 at Amazon $80 at Best Buy

Budget-conscious users would instead want to go with the HP 280 Silent Mouse. Sure, it lacks Bluetooth connectivity and has a fixed 1600 DPI, but it's a reliable and affordable pointer that can get the job done. If budget is not an issue, the Logitech MX Master 3S is another worthy alternative. While it may be a bit pricier, the mouse stands above the rest with its incredible features, including multi-device support, 8K DPI, and long battery life. The MX Keys S keyboard goes well with this mouse, and you get the two for a cheaper price by purchasing the Logitech MX Keys S Combo bundle.
item.description:
The constant clicking and scrolling sounds from a mouse may not be a big distraction for most, but they can be highly undesirable in many situations. Consider a scenario where you need to work overnight on an important project but don’t want to wake up your roommates or family members. A silent mouse is infinitely useful in this case as it can help you engage in productive tasks without worrying about disturbing your surroundings. Moreover, switching to a mouse with noise-reduction capabilities is beneficial if you prefer a noise-free working environment.

With a variety of quiet pointers available on the market, it might be difficult to pick the perfect mouse for your computing needs. To help you with your search, we've compiled a list of the best silent mice that make zero noise without sacrificing functionality or comfort.
